task_completion.description: The agent creates the conditions file with the required columns and trial distribution.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A spreadsheet, text editor, or script showing the creation of a CSV file with columns 'tone_hz', 'trigger_code', and 'is_target', containing logic or data for 300 rows (240 standard at 1000 Hz, 60 deviant at 2000 Hz).

task_completion.description: The agent configures the tone stimulus duration and jittered ISI in PsychoPy.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A Sound component properties window in PsychoPy showing a duration of 0.02 (20ms) and an ISI/start time configuration that varies between 0.9 and 1.1 seconds (900-1100ms).

task_completion.description: The agent adds a code component to print the trigger code at stimulus onset.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A Code component properties window in PsychoPy showing a print statement for the trigger code (e.g., `print(trigger_code)`) placed in the 'Begin Routine' or 'Each Frame' tab, corresponding to the stimulus onset.

task_completion.description: The agent structures the experiment with at least 2 blocks and a rest screen.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The PsychoPy Flow panel showing a trial loop nested inside an outer blocks loop, with a rest routine placed inside the blocks loop but outside the trial loop.

task_completion.description: The agent adds a final feedback screen showing hit rate and false alarm rate.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A routine at the end of the PsychoPy Flow (after the main loops) containing a Code component calculating hit rate and false alarm rate, and a Text component to display them.

task_completion.description: The agent saves the experiment and conditions file to the specified paths.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The PsychoPy window title showing `p300_oddball.psyexp` and/or a file explorer/terminal showing the files saved in `/home/ga/PsychoPyExperiments/` and `/home/ga/PsychoPyExperiments/conditions/p300_conditions.csv`.

integrity.description: The agent used the PsychoPy Builder GUI to create the experiment.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent interacting with the PsychoPy application interface (Builder view), rather than just writing an XML/Psyexp file using a text editor.

integrity.description: The conditions file was genuinely generated or edited.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent writing a script, using a spreadsheet editor, or using terminal commands to generate the CSV, rather than downloading a pre-made file or magically having it appear.

integrity.description: The agent used the exact paths requested without hallucinating directories.
integrity.visual_evidence: The file paths visible in the save dialogs or terminal should exactly match `/home/ga/PsychoPyExperiments/`.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The conditions file must have exactly 300 rows: 240 standard trials (1000 Hz) and 60 deviant trials (2000 Hz). The expected output files are /home/ga/PsychoPyExperiments/p300_oddball.psyexp and /home/ga/PsychoPyExperiments/conditions/p300_conditions.csv.
